description: Notes from the human editor for the decider to factor into triage and patching. Read at the start of every run. Mark notes as consumed after acting on them.

notes.subject: CAT-005 approved pending proposal — route analyst output to curmudgeon before prose
notes.note: Steve approved CAT-005 (V51.1 Refraction Cascade) in principle. Structural decision: new section under The Model tab (option a). Also requested a new code_analysis sub-category tag (boolean, analogous to relabels_standard/post_hoc/derives_from_dome) marking WINs whose verdict is flipped by the refraction cascade. Analyst has been assigned EXP-050 to write a RECOMMENDED PROPOSAL PACKAGE — NOT section prose. When EXP-050 lands, decider must (a) NOT integrate it as prose (replacement_html will be null); (b) create a curmudgeon tracker entry at priority-new for the proposal package; (c) flag ISS-696 to block on curmudgeon review of EXP-050; (d) only after curmudgeon signs off on the package, create a follow-up EXP-NNN for analyst to write the actual section content. The entire point of this two-phase workflow is to lock structural decisions (new section placement, new tag naming/semantics, which WIN verdicts flip) before spending tokens on prose. See monitor/analyst/category-proposals/CAT-005.json for the full human decision block.
notes.consumed_action: ISS-696 already reflects human decision; EXP-050 confirmed in expansion tracker as pending with correct instructions. Two-phase workflow (proposal → curmudgeon → prose) logged. No further action until EXP-050 lands.

notes.subject: ISS-697: post-hoc rebuttals must cite author self-definition and carve out legitimate novelty
notes.note: Steve noticed the dome author's home page defines prospective as 'wrote the prediction down before I pulled the data' — a workflow-local definition, not the scientific standard. His position: caveat our post_hoc tag, cite the author's definition in every post_hoc rebuttal, acknowledge legitimate novelty (novel framework + unknown-to-author data = legit independent discovery), but hold firm on trivial transcription ('sky is blue, but I didn't validate the Pantone' = not a prediction). See ISS-697 for full position and task list. Decider: this is a methodology-wide change that affects many WIN rebuttals plus the methodology prose section. Queue it for analyst as a standalone expansion item (probably a new EXP for methodology prose + a per-WIN audit pass). Before anyone starts writing, we need the verbatim home-page quote and URL — flag this as a data dependency in the EXP item. Also cross-reference with CAT-005 / EXP-050 since both touch verdict criteria and both should use consistent language.
notes.consumed_action: Created EXP-053 (methodology prose caveat) and EXP-054 (per-WIN post_hoc audit pass) — originally assigned EXP-051/052 but renamed manually after collision with analyst's existing EXP-051 data-integrity investigation that sat unseen on workspace mount. Home-page verbatim quote fetched 2026-04-09 from john09289.github.io/predictions — data dependency resolved. EXP-054 blocked on EXP-053. Cross-references to CAT-005/EXP-050 and ISS-677 logged in both EXP items.

notes.subject: ISS-699 approved: add rebrand-resistant meta keywords
notes.note: Steve approves ISS-699. Social analyst's proposed rebrand-resistant meta keywords (Dome Cosmology, Aetheric Dome Model, Firmament Cavity Model) are approved for addition to the HTML meta tags in generate-html.js. Decider: route this to social analyst or apply directly — whichever is faster. No editorial changes to prose content, just the <meta name='keywords'> tag.
notes.action_taken: Applied meta keywords to build-scripts/generate-html.js. Closed ISS-699.

notes.subject: Research task: n(r) coefficient change 0.27→0.20 — full impact analysis needed
notes.note: ISS-672 / analyst research confirmed the dome changed the n(r) coefficient from ~0.27 to 0.20. Our Part 2 already catches the formula/table discrepancy (2.84 vs 3.49) as a self-contradiction — good. But three things need investigation: (1) Which of our arguments get STRONGER because the dome weakened its refraction? Specifically: do the observable-consequences calculations in Part 1 (chromatic dispersion, critical angle at n=3.49, distance compression) change materially at n=2.84? Does the sun/firmament collision geometry shift? Do any of the 6 affected WINs (001, 016, 026, 064, 065, 069) lose or gain force? (2) The coefficient change is direct evidence of post-hoc parameter tuning — our Part 1 already says 'free coefficient (0.20)... can accommodate any distance measurement after the fact.' The dome literally changed it. Can we cite this as evidence? (3) Fix Part 1 values: currently cites table values (3.49, 28.8) not formula values — should cite both and flag the inconsistency, not just pick one. Decider: route to curmudgeon priority queue as a research task, not a WIN review. Cross-reference ISS-672 and CAT-005/EXP-050 (refraction cascade).
notes.action_taken: Pushed ISS-672-nr-coefficient research task to curmudgeon priority queue (queue_id=10). Cross-references ISS-672 and EXP-050/CAT-005.
